citations.desc: Primary enclosures used to transport nonhuman primates.
citations.narrative:
Images were received as part of a complaint about a nonhuman primate primary enclosure that did not have the
appropriate markings and labeling necessary for transport. The images were shown to the licensee who confirmed that
the primate was in transport. The transport enclosure was seen and confirmed to belong to the licensee during the
inspection. On the images, the primary enclosure used for transport was not marked with the words “Live Animal” or Wild
Animals” and with markings to indicate the correct upright position of the enclosure.
Failure to label transport enclosures could result in improper handling and care. Primary enclosures used to transport
nonhuman primates must be clearly marked on the top and on one or more sides with the words “Wild Animals,” or “Live
Animals.” These letters must be at least 1 inch high and have arrows or other markings to indicate the correct upright
position.
Correct by May 1, 2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representative.
n

narrative:
3.87(f) 
   
Primary enclosures used to transport nonhuman primates. 
Images were received as part of a complaint about a nonhuman primate primary enclosure that did not have the 
appropriate markings and labeling necessary for transport. The images were shown to the licensee who confirmed that 
the primate was in transport. The transport enclosure was seen and confirmed to belong to the licensee during the 
inspection. On the images, the primary enclosure used for transport was not marked with the words “Live Animal” or Wild 
Animals” and with markings to indicate the correct upright position of the enclosure. 
 
Failure to label transport enclosures could result in improper handling and care. Primary enclosures used to transport 
nonhuman primates must be clearly marked on the top and on one or more sides with the words “Wild Animals,” or “Live 
Animals.” These letters must be at least 1 inch high and have arrows or other markings to indicate the correct upright 
position. 
 
Correct by May 1, 2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representative.
